title: Seeing parent item to sub-item listed in my work
excerpt: When viewing my work, there is no way to see the parent item of a sub-item without clicking on the flow image.  SUPER ANNOYING!  I considered making a workaround via automation ->when sub-item is created, make a status a…

posts.author: SamSavage (Samantha Savage)
posts.cookedHtml: When viewing my work, there is no way to see the parent item of a sub-item without clicking on the flow image. SUPER ANNOYING! I considered making a workaround via automation ->when sub-item is created, make a status and name the status whatever the parent is, but when sub-item created isn’t an available trigger in automations. Any ideas?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml: Considering the limitations of our subitems and our subitem automations, I think we will have to get a bit creative. I wonder if the trigger “when column changes” could kickstart that status change. Then we can think of what column change could initiate the automation! What do you think?

posts.author: SusannaGP1977 (Susanna G Phillips)
posts.cookedHtml:
Charlotte, could you please clarify your response above? I don’t understand what you are saying. We also would like to see the parent name to the sub-item listed on My Work.
Happy Friday
